title: DisjointHardInterventions
lean_code_with_comments:
private lemma subset_V_of_hardInterventionOn
    {G : CDMG Node} {Wâ‚پ : Finset Node} (hWâ‚پ : Wâ‚پ âٹ† G.J âˆھ G.V)
    {Wâ‚‚ : Finset Node} (hWâ‚‚ : Wâ‚‚ âٹ† G.V) (hDisj : Disjoint Wâ‚پ Wâ‚‚) :
    Wâ‚‚ âٹ† (G.hardInterventionOn Wâ‚پ hWâ‚پ).V := by
  intro v hv
  change v âˆˆ G.V \ Wâ‚پ
  exact Finset.mem_sdiff.mpr âں¨hWâ‚‚ hv, Finset.disjoint_right.mp hDisj hvâں©

private lemma image_unsplit_subset_carrier_of_nodeSplittingOn
    {G : CDMG Node} {Wâ‚پ : Finset Node} (hWâ‚پ : Wâ‚پ âٹ† G.J âˆھ G.V)
    {Wâ‚‚ : Finset Node} (hWâ‚‚ : Wâ‚‚ âٹ† G.V) (hDisj : Disjoint Wâ‚پ Wâ‚‚) :
    Wâ‚پ.image SplitNode.unsplit âٹ†
      (G.nodeSplittingOn Wâ‚‚ hWâ‚‚).J âˆھ (G.nodeSplittingOn Wâ‚‚ hWâ‚‚).V := by
  intro x hx
  obtain âں¨v, hv, rflâں© := Finset.mem_image.mp hx
  rcases Finset.mem_union.mp (hWâ‚پ hv) with hJ | hV
  آ· -- `v âˆˆ G.J` â†’ `.unsplit v âˆˆ G.J.image .unsplit = (split).J`.
    refine Finset.mem_union_left _ ?_
    exact Finset.mem_image.mpr âں¨v, hJ, rflâں©
  آ· -- `v âˆˆ G.V`: disjointness gives `v âˆ‰ Wâ‚‚`, so `v âˆˆ G.V \ Wâ‚‚` and
    -- `.unsplit v` lands in the `(G.V \ Wâ‚‚).image .unsplit` piece of
    -- `(split).V = (G.V \ Wâ‚‚).image .unsplit âˆھ Wâ‚‚.image .copy0
    --   âˆھ Wâ‚‚.image .copy1`.
    have hv_notWâ‚‚ : v âˆ‰ Wâ‚‚ := Finset.disjoint_left.mp hDisj hv
    refine Finset.mem_union_right _ ?_
    refine Finset.mem_union_left _ ?_
    refine Finset.mem_union_left _ ?_
    exact Finset.mem_image.mpr âں¨v, Finset.mem_sdiff.mpr âں¨hV, hv_notWâ‚‚âں©, rflâں©

theorem disjointHardInterventionsAndNodeSplittingsCommute (G : CDMG Node)
    (Wâ‚پ Wâ‚‚ : Finset Node) (hWâ‚پ : Wâ‚پ âٹ† G.J âˆھ G.V) (hWâ‚‚ : Wâ‚‚ âٹ† G.V)
    (hDisj : Disjoint Wâ‚پ Wâ‚‚) :
    (G.hardInterventionOn Wâ‚پ hWâ‚پ).nodeSplittingOn Wâ‚‚
        (subset_V_of_hardInterventionOn hWâ‚پ hWâ‚‚ hDisj)
      = (G.nodeSplittingOn Wâ‚‚ hWâ‚‚).hardInterventionOn
          (Wâ‚پ.image SplitNode.unsplit)
          (image_unsplit_subset_carrier_of_nodeSplittingOn hWâ‚پ hWâ‚‚ hDisj)
-- claim_3_8 -- end statement
:= by
  -- CDMG extensionality: two CDMGs over the split-graph carrier are equal
  -- once their four data fields `(J, V, E, L)` agree.  The five
  -- propositional fields (`hJV_disj`, `hE_subset`, `hL_subset`,
  -- `hL_irrefl`, `hL_symm`) have types determined by the data fields, so
  -- proof irrelevance discharges them automatically.  Mirrors the
  -- inline-`cdmgExt` pattern of `claim_3_4`.
  have cdmgExt : âˆ€ {Gâ‚پ Gâ‚‚ : CDMG (SplitNode Node)},
      Gâ‚پ.J = Gâ‚‚.J â†’ Gâ‚پ.V = Gâ‚‚.V â†’ Gâ‚پ.E = Gâ‚‚.E â†’ Gâ‚پ.L = Gâ‚‚.L â†’ Gâ‚پ = Gâ‚‚ := by
    rintro âں¨Jâ‚پ, Vâ‚پ, hJVâ‚پ, Eâ‚پ, hEâ‚پ, Lâ‚پ, hLâ‚پ, hLiâ‚پ, hLsâ‚پâں©
           âں¨Jâ‚‚, Vâ‚‚, hJVâ‚‚, Eâ‚‚, hEâ‚‚, Lâ‚‚, hLâ‚‚, hLiâ‚‚, hLsâ‚‚âں© hJ hV hE hL
    obtain rfl := hJ
    obtain rfl := hV
    obtain rfl := hE
    obtain rfl := hL
    rfl
  -- Key membership lemma: under disjointness, the `toCopy0 Wâ‚‚`-lift of a
  -- `Node` lies outside `Wâ‚پ.image .unsplit` iff the original `Node` lies
  -- outside `Wâ‚پ`.  Implements the tex proof's "$v_k^0 \notin W_1
  -- \Leftrightarrow v_k \notin W_1$" cross-check (used both in the
  -- *directed edges* section for the `e.2` head of each generator, and
  -- twice in the *bidirected edges* section for the two endpoints of
  -- each generator).
  --
  -- Case-split on `v âˆˆ Wâ‚‚` mirrors the tex's case-split:
  --   * `v âˆˆ Wâ‚‚`: `toCopy0 Wâ‚‚ v = .copy0 v`, which is never in
  --     `Wâ‚پ.image .unsplit` by constructor mismatch; on the other side
  --     `Disjoint Wâ‚پ Wâ‚‚` rules out `v âˆˆ Wâ‚پ`.  Both sides true.
  --   * `v âˆ‰ Wâ‚‚`: `toCopy0 Wâ‚‚ v = .unsplit v`, which is in
  --     `Wâ‚پ.image .unsplit` iff `v âˆˆ Wâ‚پ` by injectivity of `.unsplit`.
  have toCopy0_notMem_iff : âˆ€ (v : Node),
      toCopy0 Wâ‚‚ v âˆ‰ Wâ‚پ.image SplitNode.unsplit â†” v âˆ‰ Wâ‚پ := by
    intro v
    unfold toCopy0
    by_cases hWâ‚‚ : v âˆˆ Wâ‚‚
    آ· rw [if_pos hWâ‚‚]
      refine âں¨fun _ hWâ‚پ => Finset.disjoint_left.mp hDisj hWâ‚پ hWâ‚‚,
              fun _ hMem => ?_âں©
      obtain âں¨_, _, hweqâں© := Finset.mem_image.mp hMem
      cases hweq
    آ· rw [if_neg hWâ‚‚]
      refine âں¨fun h hWâ‚پ => h (Finset.mem_image.mpr âں¨v, hWâ‚پ, rflâں©),
              fun h hMem => ?_âں©
      obtain âں¨w, hw, hweqâں© := Finset.mem_image.mp hMem
      cases hweq
      exact h hw
  refine cdmgExt ?_ ?_ ?_ ?_
  -- ===== Node sets: `J` =====
  -- LHS `J`: `(G.J âˆھ Wâ‚پ).image .unsplit` (after unfolding `nodeSplittingOn`
  -- applied to `G.hardInterventionOn Wâ‚پ hWâ‚پ`).
  -- RHS `J`: `G.J.image .unsplit âˆھ Wâ‚پ.image .unsplit` (after unfolding
  -- `hardInterventionOn` applied to `G.nodeSplittingOn Wâ‚‚ hWâ‚‚`).
  -- Equal by `Finset.image_union`.
  آ· change (G.J âˆھ Wâ‚پ).image SplitNode.unsplit
          = G.J.image SplitNode.unsplit âˆھ Wâ‚پ.image SplitNode.unsplit
    exact Finset.image_union _ _
  -- ===== Node sets: `V` =====
  -- LHS `V`: `((G.V \ Wâ‚پ) \ Wâ‚‚).image .unsplit âˆھ Wâ‚‚.image .copy0
  --          âˆھ Wâ‚‚.image .copy1`.
  -- RHS `V`: `((G.V \ Wâ‚‚).image .unsplit âˆھ Wâ‚‚.image .copy0
  --          âˆھ Wâ‚‚.image .copy1) \ Wâ‚پ.image .unsplit`.
  -- Per the tex's "Output nodes" section: the three pieces of the
  -- split-graph carrier decompose under set-difference with
  -- `Wâ‚پ.image .unsplit`:
  --   * `Wâ‚‚.image .copy0 \ Wâ‚پ.image .unsplit = Wâ‚‚.image .copy0`
  --     (constructor mismatch),
  --   * `Wâ‚‚.image .copy1 \ Wâ‚پ.image .unsplit = Wâ‚‚.image .copy1`
  --     (constructor mismatch),
  --   * `(G.V \ Wâ‚‚).image .unsplit \ Wâ‚پ.image .unsplit =
  --     ((G.V \ Wâ‚‚) \ Wâ‚پ).image .unsplit = ((G.V \ Wâ‚پ) \ Wâ‚‚).image .unsplit`
  --     (by injectivity of `.unsplit` and commutativity of two-step
  --     removal).
  -- We prove the equality directly via element-wise `ext` to keep the
  -- tex's case-on-constructor reading explicit.
  آ· change (((G.V \ Wâ‚پ) \ Wâ‚‚).image SplitNode.unsplit
              âˆھ Wâ‚‚.image SplitNode.copy0 âˆھ Wâ‚‚.image SplitNode.copy1)
          = ((G.V \ Wâ‚‚).image SplitNode.unsplit
              âˆھ Wâ‚‚.image SplitNode.copy0 âˆھ Wâ‚‚.image SplitNode.copy1)
            \ Wâ‚پ.image SplitNode.unsplit
    ext x
    constructor
    آ· -- LHS â†’ RHS direction.
      intro hx
      refine Finset.mem_sdiff.mpr âں¨?_, ?_âں©
      آ· -- `x` is in the inner V (RHS-pre-sdiff).
        rcases Finset.mem_union.mp hx with hx12 | hx3
        آ· rcases Finset.mem_union.mp hx12 with hx1 | hx2
          آ· -- `x = .unsplit v`, `v âˆˆ (G.V \ Wâ‚پ) \ Wâ‚‚` âٹ† `G.V \ Wâ‚‚`.
            obtain âں¨v, hv, rflâں© := Finset.mem_image.mp hx1
            obtain âں¨hv_VWâ‚پ, hv_notWâ‚‚âں© := Finset.mem_sdiff.mp hv
            obtain âں¨hv_V, _âں© := Finset.mem_sdiff.mp hv_VWâ‚پ
            refine Finset.mem_union_left _ (Finset.mem_union_left _ ?_)
            exact Finset.mem_image.mpr
              âں¨v, Finset.mem_sdiff.mpr âں¨hv_V, hv_notWâ‚‚âں©, rflâں©
          آ· refine Finset.mem_union_left _ (Finset.mem_union_right _ ?_)
            exact hx2
        آ· refine Finset.mem_union_right _ ?_
          exact hx3
      آ· -- `x âˆ‰ Wâ‚پ.image .unsplit`: case on which piece of LHS V holds x.
        rcases Finset.mem_union.mp hx with hx12 | hx3
        آ· rcases Finset.mem_union.mp hx12 with hx1 | hx2
          آ· -- `x = .unsplit v`, `v âˆ‰ Wâ‚پ` from `v âˆˆ G.V \ Wâ‚پ`.
            obtain âں¨v, hv, rflâں© := Finset.mem_image.mp hx1
            obtain âں¨hv_VWâ‚پ, _âں© := Finset.mem_sdiff.mp hv
            obtain âں¨_, hv_notWâ‚پâں© := Finset.mem_sdiff.mp hv_VWâ‚پ
            intro h
            obtain âں¨w, hw, hweqâں© := Finset.mem_image.mp h
            cases hweq
            exact hv_notWâ‚پ hw
          آ· -- `x = .copy0 w`: constructor mismatch with `.unsplit`.
            obtain âں¨w, _, rflâں© := Finset.mem_image.mp hx2
            intro h
            obtain âں¨_, _, hweqâں© := Finset.mem_image.mp h
            cases hweq
        آ· -- `x = .copy1 w`: constructor mismatch with `.unsplit`.
          obtain âں¨w, _, rflâں© := Finset.mem_image.mp hx3
          intro h
          obtain âں¨_, _, hweqâں© := Finset.mem_image.mp h
          cases hweq
    آ· -- RHS â†’ LHS direction.
      intro hx
      obtain âں¨hx_inner, hx_notWâ‚پ'âں© := Finset.mem_sdiff.mp hx
      rcases Finset.mem_union.mp hx_inner with hx12 | hx3
      آ· rcases Finset.mem_union.mp hx12 with hx1 | hx2
        آ· -- `x = .unsplit v`, `v âˆˆ G.V \ Wâ‚‚`, and `v âˆ‰ Wâ‚پ` from
          -- `hx_notWâ‚پ'` (`.unsplit v âˆ‰ Wâ‚پ.image .unsplit` by injectivity).
          obtain âں¨v, hv, rflâں© := Finset.mem_image.mp hx1
          obtain âں¨hv_V, hv_notWâ‚‚âں© := Finset.mem_sdiff.mp hv
          have hv_notWâ‚پ : v âˆ‰ Wâ‚پ := fun h =>
            hx_notWâ‚پ' (Finset.mem_image.mpr âں¨v, h, rflâں©)
          refine Finset.mem_union_left _ (Finset.mem_union_left _ ?_)
          refine Finset.mem_image.mpr âں¨v, ?_, rflâں©
          exact Finset.mem_sdiff.mpr
            âں¨Finset.mem_sdiff.mpr âں¨hv_V, hv_notWâ‚پâں©, hv_notWâ‚‚âں©
        آ· refine Finset.mem_union_left _ (Finset.mem_union_right _ ?_)
          exact hx2
      آ· refine Finset.mem_union_right _ ?_
        exact hx3
  -- ===== Directed edges: `E` =====
  -- LHS `E`: `(G.E.filter (e.2 âˆ‰ Wâ‚پ)).image (toCopy1 Wâ‚‚ آ·.1, toCopy0 Wâ‚‚ آ·.2)
  --          âˆھ Wâ‚‚.image (آ·.copy0, آ·.copy1)`.
  -- RHS `E`: `(G.E.image (toCopy1 Wâ‚‚ آ·.1, toCopy0 Wâ‚‚ آ·.2)
  --          âˆھ Wâ‚‚.image (آ·.copy0, آ·.copy1)).filter (e.2 âˆ‰ Wâ‚پ.image .unsplit)`.
  -- Per the tex's "Directed edges" section:
  --   * Push the outer `.filter` through the union with `Finset.filter_union`.
  --   * For the lifted-`G.E` piece: `Finset.filter_image` swaps to a
  --     pre-image-filter form, and the predicate matches `e.2 âˆ‰ Wâ‚پ` via
  --     `toCopy0_notMem_iff` applied to `e.2`.
  --   * For the transfer-edge piece `Wâ‚‚.image (آ·.copy0, آ·.copy1)`: the
  --     head `.copy1 w` of each transfer edge is never in `Wâ‚پ.image
  --     .unsplit` (constructor mismatch), so the filter is vacuous and
  --     leaves the set unchanged.
  آ· change ((G.E.filter (fun e : Node أ— Node => e.2 âˆ‰ Wâ‚پ)).image
              (fun e : Node أ— Node => (toCopy1 Wâ‚‚ e.1, toCopy0 Wâ‚‚ e.2))
            âˆھ Wâ‚‚.image (fun w : Node => (SplitNode.copy0 w, SplitNode.copy1 w)))
          = (G.E.image
                (fun e : Node أ— Node => (toCopy1 Wâ‚‚ e.1, toCopy0 Wâ‚‚ e.2))
              âˆھ Wâ‚‚.image
                (fun w : Node => (SplitNode.copy0 w, SplitNode.copy1 w))).filter
              (fun e : SplitNode Node أ— SplitNode Node =>
                e.2 âˆ‰ Wâ‚پ.image SplitNode.unsplit)
    rw [Finset.filter_union, Finset.filter_image]
    congr 1
    آ· -- Lifted-`G.E` piece: filter-pred agreement under `Finset.filter_congr`.
      congr 1
      refine Finset.filter_congr ?_
      intro e he
      exact (toCopy0_notMem_iff e.2).symm
    آ· -- Transfer-edge piece: filter is vacuous on `Wâ‚‚.image (آ·.copy0, آ·.copy1)`.
      symm
      refine Finset.filter_true_of_mem ?_
      intro x hx
      obtain âں¨w, _, rflâں© := Finset.mem_image.mp hx
      intro h
      obtain âں¨_, _, hweqâں© := Finset.mem_image.mp h
      cases hweq
  -- ===== Bidirected edges: `L` =====
  -- LHS `L`: `(G.L.filter (e.1 âˆ‰ Wâ‚پ âˆ§ e.2 âˆ‰ Wâ‚پ)).image
  --          (toCopy0 Wâ‚‚ آ·.1, toCopy0 Wâ‚‚ آ·.2)`.
  -- RHS `L`: `(G.L.image (toCopy0 Wâ‚‚ آ·.1, toCopy0 Wâ‚‚ آ·.2)).filter
  --          (e.1 âˆ‰ Wâ‚پ.image .unsplit âˆ§ e.2 âˆ‰ Wâ‚پ.image .unsplit)`.
  -- Per the tex's "Bidirected edges" section: `Finset.filter_image`
  -- swaps to a pre-image-filter form, and `toCopy0_notMem_iff` applies
  -- to both endpoints (the bidirected-edge case has no transfer-edge
  -- analogue â€” `def_3_11` item iv has a single image clause).  The
  -- two-sided filter convention here is the registered deviation
  -- `hard_intervention_l_symmetrized_removal` from `def_3_10`; per the
  -- tex's "Registered two-sided removal of `L`" paragraph, the two-sided
  -- and LN-literal one-sided readings agree under `L`'s symmetry axiom,
  -- so the tex's iff `v_k^0 âˆ‰ W_1 â†” v_k âˆ‰ W_1` applied to both `k = 1, 2`
  -- closes the goal.
  آ· change (G.L.filter (fun e : Node أ— Node => e.1 âˆ‰ Wâ‚پ âˆ§ e.2 âˆ‰ Wâ‚پ)).image
              (fun e : Node أ— Node => (toCopy0 Wâ‚‚ e.1, toCopy0 Wâ‚‚ e.2))
          = (G.L.image
                (fun e : Node أ— Node => (toCopy0 Wâ‚‚ e.1, toCopy0 Wâ‚‚ e.2))).filter
              (fun e : SplitNode Node أ— SplitNode Node =>
                e.1 âˆ‰ Wâ‚پ.image SplitNode.unsplit
                  âˆ§ e.2 âˆ‰ Wâ‚پ.image SplitNode.unsplit)
    rw [Finset.filter_image]
    congr 1
    refine Finset.filter_congr ?_
    intro e he
    constructor
    آ· rintro âں¨h1, h2âں©
      exact âں¨(toCopy0_notMem_iff e.1).mpr h1, (toCopy0_notMem_iff e.2).mpr h2âں©
    آ· rintro âں¨h1, h2âں©
      exact âں¨(toCopy0_notMem_iff e.1).mp h1, (toCopy0_notMem_iff e.2).mp h2âں©
lean_code_without_comments:
private lemma subset_V_of_hardInterventionOn
    {G : CDMG Node} {Wâ‚پ : Finset Node} (hWâ‚پ : Wâ‚پ âٹ† G.J âˆھ G.V)
    {Wâ‚‚ : Finset Node} (hWâ‚‚ : Wâ‚‚ âٹ† G.V) (hDisj : Disjoint Wâ‚پ Wâ‚‚) :
    Wâ‚‚ âٹ† (G.hardInterventionOn Wâ‚پ hWâ‚پ).V := by
  intro v hv
  change v âˆˆ G.V \ Wâ‚پ
  exact Finset.mem_sdiff.mpr âں¨hWâ‚‚ hv, Finset.disjoint_right.mp hDisj hvâں©

private lemma image_unsplit_subset_carrier_of_nodeSplittingOn
    {G : CDMG Node} {Wâ‚پ : Finset Node} (hWâ‚پ : Wâ‚پ âٹ† G.J âˆھ G.V)
    {Wâ‚‚ : Finset Node} (hWâ‚‚ : Wâ‚‚ âٹ† G.V) (hDisj : Disjoint Wâ‚پ Wâ‚‚) :
    Wâ‚پ.image SplitNode.unsplit âٹ†
      (G.nodeSplittingOn Wâ‚‚ hWâ‚‚).J âˆھ (G.nodeSplittingOn Wâ‚‚ hWâ‚‚).V := by
  intro x hx
  obtain âں¨v, hv, rflâں© := Finset.mem_image.mp hx
  rcases Finset.mem_union.mp (hWâ‚پ hv) with hJ | hV
  آ·
    refine Finset.mem_union_left _ ?_
    exact Finset.mem_image.mpr âں¨v, hJ, rflâں©
  آ·

    have hv_notWâ‚‚ : v âˆ‰ Wâ‚‚ := Finset.disjoint_left.mp hDisj hv
    refine Finset.mem_union_right _ ?_
    refine Finset.mem_union_left _ ?_
    refine Finset.mem_union_left _ ?_
    exact Finset.mem_image.mpr âں¨v, Finset.mem_sdiff.mpr âں¨hV, hv_notWâ‚‚âں©, rflâں©

theorem disjointHardInterventionsAndNodeSplittingsCommute (G : CDMG Node)
    (Wâ‚پ Wâ‚‚ : Finset Node) (hWâ‚پ : Wâ‚پ âٹ† G.J âˆھ G.V) (hWâ‚‚ : Wâ‚‚ âٹ† G.V)
    (hDisj : Disjoint Wâ‚پ Wâ‚‚) :
    (G.hardInterventionOn Wâ‚پ hWâ‚پ).nodeSplittingOn Wâ‚‚
        (subset_V_of_hardInterventionOn hWâ‚پ hWâ‚‚ hDisj)
      = (G.nodeSplittingOn Wâ‚‚ hWâ‚‚).hardInterventionOn
          (Wâ‚پ.image SplitNode.unsplit)
          (image_unsplit_subset_carrier_of_nodeSplittingOn hWâ‚پ hWâ‚‚ hDisj)

:= by

  have cdmgExt : âˆ€ {Gâ‚پ Gâ‚‚ : CDMG (SplitNode Node)},
      Gâ‚پ.J = Gâ‚‚.J â†’ Gâ‚پ.V = Gâ‚‚.V â†’ Gâ‚پ.E = Gâ‚‚.E â†’ Gâ‚پ.L = Gâ‚‚.L â†’ Gâ‚پ = Gâ‚‚ := by
    rintro âں¨Jâ‚پ, Vâ‚پ, hJVâ‚پ, Eâ‚پ, hEâ‚پ, Lâ‚پ, hLâ‚پ, hLiâ‚پ, hLsâ‚پâں©
           âں¨Jâ‚‚, Vâ‚‚, hJVâ‚‚, Eâ‚‚, hEâ‚‚, Lâ‚‚, hLâ‚‚, hLiâ‚‚, hLsâ‚‚âں© hJ hV hE hL
    obtain rfl := hJ
    obtain rfl := hV
    obtain rfl := hE
    obtain rfl := hL
    rfl

  have toCopy0_notMem_iff : âˆ€ (v : Node),
      toCopy0 Wâ‚‚ v âˆ‰ Wâ‚پ.image SplitNode.unsplit â†” v âˆ‰ Wâ‚پ := by
    intro v
    unfold toCopy0
    by_cases hWâ‚‚ : v âˆˆ Wâ‚‚
    آ· rw [if_pos hWâ‚‚]
      refine âں¨fun _ hWâ‚پ => Finset.disjoint_left.mp hDisj hWâ‚پ hWâ‚‚,
              fun _ hMem => ?_âں©
      obtain âں¨_, _, hweqâں© := Finset.mem_image.mp hMem
      cases hweq
    آ· rw [if_neg hWâ‚‚]
      refine âں¨fun h hWâ‚پ => h (Finset.mem_image.mpr âں¨v, hWâ‚پ, rflâں©),
              fun h hMem => ?_âں©
      obtain âں¨w, hw, hweqâں© := Finset.mem_image.mp hMem
      cases hweq
      exact h hw
  refine cdmgExt ?_ ?_ ?_ ?_

  آ· change (G.J âˆھ Wâ‚پ).image SplitNode.unsplit
          = G.J.image SplitNode.unsplit âˆھ Wâ‚پ.image SplitNode.unsplit
    exact Finset.image_union _ _

  آ· change (((G.V \ Wâ‚پ) \ Wâ‚‚).image SplitNode.unsplit
              âˆھ Wâ‚‚.image SplitNode.copy0 âˆھ Wâ‚‚.image SplitNode.copy1)
          = ((G.V \ Wâ‚‚).image SplitNode.unsplit
              âˆھ Wâ‚‚.image SplitNode.copy0 âˆھ Wâ‚‚.image SplitNode.copy1)
            \ Wâ‚پ.image SplitNode.unsplit
    ext x
    constructor
    آ·
      intro hx
      refine Finset.mem_sdiff.mpr âں¨?_, ?_âں©
      آ·
        rcases Finset.mem_union.mp hx with hx12 | hx3
        آ· rcases Finset.mem_union.mp hx12 with hx1 | hx2
          آ·
            obtain âں¨v, hv, rflâں© := Finset.mem_image.mp hx1
            obtain âں¨hv_VWâ‚پ, hv_notWâ‚‚âں© := Finset.mem_sdiff.mp hv
            obtain âں¨hv_V, _âں© := Finset.mem_sdiff.mp hv_VWâ‚پ
            refine Finset.mem_union_left _ (Finset.mem_union_left _ ?_)
            exact Finset.mem_image.mpr
              âں¨v, Finset.mem_sdiff.mpr âں¨hv_V, hv_notWâ‚‚âں©, rflâں©
          آ· refine Finset.mem_union_left _ (Finset.mem_union_right _ ?_)
            exact hx2
        آ· refine Finset.mem_union_right _ ?_
          exact hx3
      آ·
        rcases Finset.mem_union.mp hx with hx12 | hx3
        آ· rcases Finset.mem_union.mp hx12 with hx1 | hx2
          آ·
            obtain âں¨v, hv, rflâں© := Finset.mem_image.mp hx1
            obtain âں¨hv_VWâ‚پ, _âں© := Finset.mem_sdiff.mp hv
            obtain âں¨_, hv_notWâ‚پâں© := Finset.mem_sdiff.mp hv_VWâ‚پ
            intro h
            obtain âں¨w, hw, hweqâں© := Finset.mem_image.mp h
            cases hweq
            exact hv_notWâ‚پ hw
          آ·
            obtain âں¨w, _, rflâں© := Finset.mem_image.mp hx2
            intro h
            obtain âں¨_, _, hweqâں© := Finset.mem_image.mp h
            cases hweq
        آ·
          obtain âں¨w, _, rflâں© := Finset.mem_image.mp hx3
          intro h
          obtain âں¨_, _, hweqâں© := Finset.mem_image.mp h
          cases hweq
    آ·
      intro hx
      obtain âں¨hx_inner, hx_notWâ‚پ'âں© := Finset.mem_sdiff.mp hx
      rcases Finset.mem_union.mp hx_inner with hx12 | hx3
      آ· rcases Finset.mem_union.mp hx12 with hx1 | hx2
        آ·

          obtain âں¨v, hv, rflâں© := Finset.mem_image.mp hx1
          obtain âں¨hv_V, hv_notWâ‚‚âں© := Finset.mem_sdiff.mp hv
          have hv_notWâ‚پ : v âˆ‰ Wâ‚پ := fun h =>

          exact Finset.mem_sdiff.mpr
            âں¨Finset.mem_sdiff.mpr âں¨hv_V, hv_notWâ‚پâں©, hv_notWâ‚‚âں©
        آ· refine Finset.mem_union_left _ (Finset.mem_union_right _ ?_)

  آ· change ((G.E.filter (fun e : Node أ— Node => e.2 âˆ‰ Wâ‚پ)).image
              (fun e : Node أ— Node => (toCopy1 Wâ‚‚ e.1, toCopy0 Wâ‚‚ e.2))
            âˆھ Wâ‚‚.image (fun w : Node => (SplitNode.copy0 w, SplitNode.copy1 w)))

lean_explanation:
This row formalises the LN equality $(G_{\mathrm{doit}(W_1)})_{\mathrm{spl}(W_2)} = (G_{\mathrm{spl}(W_2)})_{\mathrm{doit}(W_1)}$ for a CDMG `G` and two disjoint subsets $W_1 \subseteq J \cup V$, $W_2 \subseteq V$. The binder `{Node : Type*}` with `[DecidableEq Node]` is the ambient vertex type â€” the LN's silent choice of carrier, with decidability needed so the four `Finset`-valued fields `J`, `V`, `E`, `L` admit membership tests. `G : CDMG Node` is the input graph; `Wâ‚پ Wâ‚‚ : Finset Node` are the LN's $W_1$, $W_2$; `hWâ‚پ`, `hWâ‚‚` capture the typings $W_1 \subseteq J \cup V$ and $W_2 \subseteq V$; `hDisj : Disjoint Wâ‚پ Wâ‚‚` is the Mathlib-idiomatic form of $W_1 \cap W_2 = \emptyset$.

`G.hardInterventionOn Wâ‚پ hWâ‚پ` realises the LN's $G_{\mathrm{doit}(W_1)}$ (def 3.10), which preserves the node carrier as `Node`. `G.nodeSplittingOn Wâ‚‚ hWâ‚‚` realises $G_{\mathrm{spl}(W_2)}$ (def 3.11), which *lifts* the carrier to `SplitNode Node` â€” an inductive type with three constructors: `.unsplit` for nodes untouched by the split, and `.copy0` / `.copy1` for the two tagged copies of nodes inside $W_2$. Both sides of the asserted equality therefore land in `CDMG (SplitNode Node)`, and the equality is a literal `=` between two terms of identical Lean type. On the RHS, the LN's reading of $\mathrm{doit}(W_1)$ as 'hard-intervention on the unsplit copies of $W_1$ inside the split-graph carrier' is rendered as `Wâ‚پ.image SplitNode.unsplit`, lifting each $w \in W_1$ to its unsplit copy.

The Mathlib idiom `Disjoint Wâ‚پ Wâ‚‚` is the canonical `Finset` form of disjointness; it feeds `Finset.disjoint_left.mp` and `Finset.disjoint_right.mp` directly. `Finset.image SplitNode.unsplit` is the standard image of a `Finset` along a (constructor) function. Two private helpers discharge the well-typedness side conditions of the iterated-construction signatures: `subset_V_of_hardInterventionOn` proves $W_2 \subseteq V \setminus W_1$ (the precondition of the LHS's outer splitting), and `image_unsplit_subset_carrier_of_nodeSplittingOn` proves that the unsplit lift `Wâ‚پ.image SplitNode.unsplit` lands inside the split-graph carrier (the precondition of the RHS's outer intervention). The theorem itself takes `G`, `Wâ‚پ`, `Wâ‚‚`, the three subset/disjointness hypotheses, and concludes the literal CDMG equality.
design_choices:
**Literal `=` of CDMGs, not `eqViaNodeMap` / `flattenSplit`.** This is the dominant simplification relative to `claim_3_7` (`TwoDisjointNode`). Both sides take a *single* node-splitting on the same `Wâ‚‚`, and `hardInterventionOn` preserves the node carrier per def 3.10, so both sides land in `CDMG (SplitNode Node)`: no carrier mismatch arises and the asserted equality is a literal `=` between two terms of identical Lean type. Contrast `claim_3_7`, where iterating `nodeSplittingOn` twice produces `CDMG (SplitNode (SplitNode Node))` with disagreeing constructor wrappings, forcing a canonical-flatten relabelling. Here the LN's 'the same CDMG' reading is delivered by Lean's structural `=`; no carrier-relabelling map, no quotient types. Mirrors the literal-`=` pattern of `claim_3_4` (`HardInterventionsCommute`), lifted to the `SplitNode Node` carrier.

**Disjointness `Disjoint Wâ‚پ Wâ‚‚` is genuinely load-bearing.** Two distinct uses appear inside the signature: the LHS's inner splitting needs $W_2 \subseteq V \setminus W_1$ (discharged by `subset_V_of_hardInterventionOn`), and the RHS's outer intervention needs the unsplit-lifted $W_1$ to sit inside the split-graph carrier (discharged by `image_unsplit_subset_carrier_of_nodeSplittingOn`). Without disjointness, a node $w \in W_1 \cap W_2$ would simultaneously be hard-intervened (becomes an input on one side) and split (gets new copies on the other), committing both sides to incompatible carrier placements; the equality would no longer hold set-theoretically. Encoded as Mathlib's `Disjoint` rather than a raw `Finset.inter` equality, so the helper proofs are one step from `Finset.disjoint_left.mp` / `_right.mp`.

**Lift `Wâ‚پ` to the split carrier via `Finset.image SplitNode.unsplit`.** Every $w \in W_1$ lies in $J \cup (V \setminus W_2)$ by `hWâ‚پ` and disjointness â€” the untagged piece of the split-graph carrier under def 3.11's shorthand $v^0 := v^1 := v$. Lifting via `.image .unsplit` lets the RHS's outer hard intervention be expressed in terms of the *original* `Wâ‚پ`, rather than forcing the consumer to construct a fresh `Finset (SplitNode Node)` from scratch. The same lift convention is used in `claim_3_7`; reusing it here keeps the two formalisations parallel.

**Single theorem, not a conjunction.** The LN statement is a single equality $\mathrm{LHS} = \mathrm{RHS}$, not a triple equality of the kind that `claim_3_4` and `claim_3_7` decomposed into `(a) âˆ§ (b)`. There is no joint-intervention form analogous to $G_{\mathrm{do}(W_1 \cup W_2)}$ available here, because `doit` and `spl` are structurally distinct operations (different carriers, different field equations) and do not admit a single combined invocation.

**Two-sided filter on $L$ (registered deviation).** The encoding of $\mathrm{doit}(W)$ in def 3.10 filters $L_{\mathrm{doit}(W)}$ two-sidedly â€” removing pairs whenever *either* endpoint lies in $W$ â€” instead of the LN's literal one-sided clause. The one-sided form does not preserve $L$'s symmetry axiom, since for $w \in W$ and $v \notin W$ the symmetric partners $(v, w)$ and $(w, v)$ would otherwise be treated asymmetrically. Under the symmetry axiom both readings collapse to the same survivor set, so the bidirected-edge component is unaffected by the choice.
